seo.title: Building Data Extraction with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building data extraction agents with OpenAI Assistants API enables schema-guided extraction from unstructured documents using built-in tools like file search and function calling. However, hallucinations in schema compliance, incomplete field extraction, and context overflow can lead to unreliable outputs. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure schema accuracy, completeness, and type consistency before production use.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with uploading documents to a thread via the Assistants API. An assistant configured with a JSON schema for extraction (e.g., invoice data) processes files using file search and code interpreter tools. The assistant generates structured JSON output through function calling or direct response.

Runs are triggered asynchronously, with webhooks capturing completion events containing extracted data. Verification logic validates output against the schema before persisting to downstream systems like databases or APIs. Thread management prevents context bloat by pruning old messages.

This setup leverages OpenAI's managed runtime while adding Vex for output validation, ensuring high-fidelity extraction at scale.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌─────────────────┐
│   Unstructured  │───▶│ OpenAI Assistants│───▶│   Vex           │
│     Documents   │    │ - File Search    │    │   Verification  │
└─────────────────┘    │ - Code Interpreter│    │ - Schema Check  │
                       │ - Function Calling│    │ - Completeness  │
                       └──────────┬─────────┘    └────────┬────────┘
                                  │                      │
                       ┌──────────▼─────────┐    ┌────────▼────────┐
                       │   Thread/Run       │    │ Validated JSON  │
                       │   Completion       │◀───│   Output        │
                       │   Webhook Event    │    │ (Invoice Data)  │
                       └────────────────────┘    └─────────────────┘

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Assistant may generate malicious code during data parsing (e.g., file writes, network calls)
risks.mitigation: Vex sandbox isolation + operation whitelisting on run completion

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Incorrect file retrieval leads to extraction from wrong documents
risks.mitigation: Vex relevance scoring + document timestamp validation

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Excessive messages cause context window overflow and degraded extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex thread pruning + message count limits before run completion

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Extracted JSON fields missing, wrong types, or hallucinated values
risks.mitigation: Vex JSON schema validation + field completeness checks

implementation.title: Create Assistant with Extraction Schema
implementation.description: Configure assistant with JSON schema for invoice extraction and file search enabled

implementation.title: Upload Documents and Create Thread
implementation.description: Upload PDF invoices and create thread for processing
implementation.code:
file = client.files.create(
    file=open("invoice.pdf", "rb"),
    purpose="assistants"
)

thread = client.beta.threads.create()

client.beta.threads.messages.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    role="user",
    content="Extract structured data from this invoice.",
    file_ids=[file.id]
)

implementation.title: TypeScript: Schema-Guided Function Calling

vexIntegration.title: Python: Vex Webhook Schema Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify extraction output against JSON schema on run completion
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from flask import Flask, request

app = Flask(__name__)
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

@app.route('/webhook/assistant', methods=['POST'])
def verify_extraction():
    event = request.json
    if event['event'] == 'thread.run.completed':
        thread_id = event['data']['thread_id']
        
        # Get latest message
        messages = openai.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
        extraction = json.loads(messages.data[0].content[0].text.value)
        
        # Vex verification
        result = vex_client.verify({
            "name": "invoice-extraction",
            "input": extraction,
            "rules": [
                vex.schema("extraction", invoice_schema),
                vex.field_present("invoice_number"),
                vex.type_check("total", "number"),
                vex.range_check("total", min=0)
            ]
        })
        
        if result.valid:
            save_to_db(extraction)
    return 'OK', 200
